archetype: Automated Radiation Therapy Treatment Planning and Quality Assurance System for Proton Therapy Centers
description: This system generates optimized radiation treatment plans for cancer patients undergoing proton therapy, ensuring precise dose delivery while minimizing damage to surrounding tissues. Radiation oncologists and physicists use the system to create and verify treatment plans, which must comply with strict regulations and protocols. The system's complexity arises from integrating diverse data sources, such as patient anatomy, tumor characteristics, and machine-specific radiation delivery parameters.
rules: MUST comply with IEC 61217 and ICRU 78 standards for radiation therapy treatment planning
MUST integrate with Varian, Siemens, or other proton therapy machine control systems
MUST account for variations in patient anatomy and tumor motion during treatment
MUST ensure accurate dose calculation and verification using Monte Carlo simulations or pencil beam algorithms
MUST maintain a comprehensive audit trail for regulatory compliance and quality assurance
anti_patterns: DO NOT use a generic 3D visualization library without accounting for radiation therapy-specific requirements
DO NOT neglect to incorporate machine-specific radiation delivery parameters
DO NOT overlook the need for robust patient data management and version control
key_technical_challenges: Developing an efficient and accurate dose calculation engine
Integrating with diverse machine control systems and data formats
Implementing robust data management and version control for patient and treatment plan data

recommended_stack.language: C++ for performance-critical dose calculation and simulation components
recommended_stack.framework: Qt for cross-platform GUI development and user interface consistency
recommended_stack.database_engine: PostgreSQL for robust data management and version control
recommended_stack.cache: Redis for caching frequently accessed patient and treatment plan data
recommended_stack.message_broker: RabbitMQ for integrating with machine control systems and other external services
